Command missing allowed-tools declaration; principle of least privilege not enforced; no restriction prevents accidental use of Write or Edit
All 9 speckit command files share the same missing-name: defect indicating a scaffolding template gap rather than per-file oversights

eval on output that includes the SPECIFY_FEATURE environment variable enables shell injection when the env var contains single-quote characters
eval on output containing SPECIFY_FEATURE env var; successful injection can backdoor agent context files written by this script
CLAUDE.md Commands section has unfilled template placeholders from incomplete auto-generation; commands section is non-functional for developers
Three duplicate technology stack entries in CLAUDE.md from repeated auto-generation runs without deduplication; creates misleading impression of more active features
CLAUDE.md malformation is caused by a missing global flag in update-agent-context.sh sed substitution; cross-component causal link between script bug and CLAUDE.md content bug
PyYAML dependency uses non-pinned lower-bound constraint; future versions introducing breaking changes or vulnerabilities would be silently adopted
--output CLI argument passed to open() without path canonicalization; allows writing to arbitrary filesystem paths such as ../../.claude/commands/injected.md
GITHUB_TOKEN appended to Authorization header for every HTTP call including config-sourced URLs; supply chain compromise of config.yaml or upstream awesome-repo-configs exfiltrates the token

Missing required name: frontmatter field; command is unindexable by NLPM registry tooling — frontmatter block contains description: and handoffs: fields; name: key is absent
Missing required name: frontmatter field; command is unindexable by NLPM registry tooling — frontmatter block contains description: and tools: fields; name: key is absent

CLAUDE.md Commands section has unfilled template placeholders from incomplete auto-generation; commands section is non-functional for developers — Commands section contains literal string [ONLY COMMANDS FOR ACTIVE TECHNOLOGIES] four times interspersed with valid command fragments; sed substitution in update-agent-context.sh line 337 lacks g flag causing partial replacement

GITHUB_TOKEN appended to Authorization header for every HTTP call including config-sourced URLs; supply chain compromise of config.yaml or upstream awesome-repo-configs exfiltrates the token — _jget() at line 14 adds Authorization: Bearer {token} to all requests; fetch_repos_from_sources() at line 22 calls _jget(source['url'], token) where source['url'] is attacker-controllable via config.yaml; no domain validation present in codebase

eval on output that includes the SPECIFY_FEATURE environment variable enables shell injection when the env var contains single-quote characters — eval $(get_feature_paths) evaluated; get_feature_paths() interpolates $SPECIFY_FEATURE env var into single-quoted heredoc lines; a value containing ' breaks quoting and executes injected commands

eval on output containing SPECIFY_FEATURE env var; successful injection can backdoor agent context files written by this script — eval $(get_feature_paths) at line 56; same pattern as check-prerequisites.sh; additionally this script writes to CLAUDE.md and all agent context files, amplifying injection impact

--output CLI argument passed to open() without path canonicalization; allows writing to arbitrary filesystem paths such as ../../.claude/commands/injected.md — generate_readme() at line 92 calls open(output_file, 'w') where output_file comes directly from args.output (CLI --output flag) without path validation or canonicalization

PyYAML dependency uses non-pinned lower-bound constraint; future versions introducing breaking changes or vulnerabilities would be silently adopted — requirements.txt line 1 contains PyYAML>=6.0; >= constraint allows any future major version to be installed silently

Command missing allowed-tools declaration; principle of least privilege not enforced; no restriction prevents accidental use of Write or Edit — frontmatter has no tools: or allowed-tools: key; command runs Bash (check-prerequisites.sh) and Read but declares no tool surface
Command missing allowed-tools declaration; tool surface is unconstrained — frontmatter has no tools: or allowed-tools: key; command uses Bash, Read, and Write to create checklist files
Command missing allowed-tools declaration; tool surface is unconstrained — frontmatter has no tools: or allowed-tools: key; command uses Bash, Read, and Write
Command missing allowed-tools declaration; tool surface is unconstrained — frontmatter has no tools: or allowed-tools: key; command uses Bash, Read, and Write
Command missing allowed-tools declaration; tool surface is unconstrained — frontmatter has no tools: or allowed-tools: key; command uses Bash, Read, Write, and Edit
Command missing allowed-tools declaration; tool surface is unconstrained — frontmatter has no tools: or allowed-tools: key; command uses Bash, Read, and Write
Command missing allowed-tools declaration; tool surface is unconstrained — frontmatter has no tools: or allowed-tools: key; command uses Bash, Read, and Write
Command missing allowed-tools declaration; tool surface is unconstrained — frontmatter has no tools: or allowed-tools: key; command uses Bash, Read, and Write

6 vague quantifiers across speckit.specify.md (reasonable x3, appropriate, user-friendly, meaningful) remove measurable acceptance criteria from the specification workflow — line 232 contains 'User-friendly messages with appropriate fallbacks'; lines 83/219/229 each contain 'reasonable' without a measurable definition; line 28 uses 'most meaningful keywords'

'specific enough' is a vague qualifier; no measurable threshold defined for task specificity — line 65: 'specific enough that an LLM can complete it without additional context' provides no objective threshold for specificity

Three duplicate technology stack entries in CLAUDE.md from repeated auto-generation runs without deduplication; creates misleading impression of more active features — Active Technologies section and Recent Changes section each contain three near-identical entries for the same 001-marketplace-scraper feature with only minor wording variation

All 9 speckit command files share the same missing-name: defect indicating a scaffolding template gap rather than per-file oversights — All 9 .claude/commands/speckit.*.md files missing name: field; the agent-file template in .specify/templates/ omits this key; single template fix resolves all 9 instances

CLAUDE.md malformation is caused by a missing global flag in update-agent-context.sh sed substitution; cross-component causal link between script bug and CLAUDE.md content bug — update-agent-context.sh line 337 sed substitution s|\[ONLY COMMANDS FOR ACTIVE TECHNOLOGIES\]|$commands| lacks g flag; template has placeholder multiple times; each run replaces only first occurrence leaving orphaned placeholders interspersed with real commands
